quotes.quote: After idea generation, we filter ideas by connecting the language model with the Semantic Scholar API (Fricke, 2018) and web access as a tool (Schick et al., 2024). This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.
quotes.rationale: Implemented prior-art comparison that discards ideas, i.e. a stage gate on novelty.

quotes.quote: After the completion of each experiment, Aider is then given the results and told to take notes in the style of an experimental journal. Currently, it only conditions on text but in future versions, this could include data visualizations or any modality. Conditional on the results, it then re-plans and implements the next experiment. This process is repeated up to five times.
quotes.rationale: Experimental results are routed back into re-planning of subsequent experiments, i.e. closed computational search control.

quotes.quote: To evaluate the LLM-based reviewer’s performance, we compared the artificially generated decisions with ground truth data for 500 ICLR 2022 papers extracted from the publicly available OpenReview dataset (Berto, 2024).
quotes.rationale: Reviewer decisions benchmarked against real conference ground-truth decisions.

quotes.quote: Review ensembling (5 reviews) and subsequent meta-aggregation, on the other hand, did not affect the reviewer’s performance, but can reduce variance.
quotes.rationale: Reported null effect of ensembling on measured reviewer performance.

quotes.quote: Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.
quotes.rationale: Stated limitation of the reviewer's evidence access.

quotes.quote: Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.
quotes.rationale: Explicitly absent capability described only as a future possibility.

quotes.quote: Finally, The AI Scientist adds the completed ideas and reviewer feedback to its archive of scientific findings, and the process repeats.
quotes.rationale: Completed ideas and reviewer output are admitted to a persistent archive reused across repetitions.

policy_type: LLM-driven iterative re-planning conditioned on prior experiment results, bounded at up to five experiment iterations
Prior-art similarity gate discarding overly similar ideas before downstream stages
memory_structure: Archive of scientific findings containing completed ideas and reviewer feedback, reused across repetitions
Experimental journal notes recorded after each experiment

diagnostics.statement: The implemented novelty gate discards ideas judged too similar to existing literature, but no reliability study of that similarity judgment is reported in the packet.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.

diagnostics.consequence: Irreversible discarding of candidate ideas rests on an unvalidated novelty judgment, so false rejections and false admissions are undetectable.

diagnostics.statement: Reviewer benchmarking against 500 ICLR 2022 papers is a discrimination study of aggregate accept/reject quality, and ensembling with meta-aggregation did not improve measured performance.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: we compared the artificially generated decisions with ground truth data for 500 ICLR 2022 papers extracted from the publicly available OpenReview dataset (Berto, 2024).
Review ensembling (5 reviews) and subsequent meta-aggregation, on the other hand, did not affect the reviewer’s performance, but can reduce variance.

diagnostics.consequence: Aggregate-quality evidence does not extend to novelty or empirical adequacy of generated work, and adding reviews does not close the gap.

diagnostics.statement: The reviewer operates without vision and cannot query authors, and the authors explicitly disclaim face-value acceptance of the generated scientific content.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.
the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.
we do not recommend taking the scientific content of this version of The AI Scientist at face value.

diagnostics.consequence: Terminal assessment cannot substitute for external verification; downstream human follow-up remains required for any claim of correctness.

diagnostics.statement: Reviewer feedback is written to the archive, but the packet does not state whether or how archived feedback constrains later idea generation or experiment planning.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The AI Scientist adds the completed ideas and reviewer feedback to its archive of scientific findings, and the process repeats.
Conditional on the results, it then re-plans and implements the next experiment. This process is repeated up to five times.

diagnostics.consequence: Closure of the reviewer-to-policy loop cannot be established; the reviewer may be effectively terminal despite archival storage.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Instead, we advise treating generated papers as hints of promising ideas for practitioners to follow up on.

To evaluate the LLM-based reviewer’s performance, we compared the artificially generated decisions with ground truth data for 500 ICLR 2022 papers extracted from the publicly available OpenReview dataset (Berto, 2024).

success_check.observable: Independent verification of generated papers' empirical claims by an evaluator outside the generating pipeline
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Verified claim rate reported against the internal reviewer's accept decisions on the same papers
success_check.evidence_needed: Independent re-execution or external expert audit records for a defined sample of generated papers

exact_quotes.exact_quote: After idea generation, we filter ideas by connecting the language model with the Semantic Scholar API (Fricke, 2018) and web access as a tool (Schick et al., 2024). This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.

success_check.observable: Error rates of the similarity gate on a labelled set of novel and non-novel candidate ideas
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: False-discard and false-admit rates reported against human novelty labels
success_check.evidence_needed: A held-out labelled idea set with gate decisions logged per idea

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Finally, The AI Scientist adds the completed ideas and reviewer feedback to its archive of scientific findings, and the process repeats.

success_check.observable: Documented routing of archived reviewer feedback into subsequent ideation or planning prompts
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Explicit statement of whether archive contents condition later decisions, or explicit none
success_check.evidence_needed: Pipeline description or trace showing archive reads at the next iteration
